data.heading: FuzzyTime
data.text: An instant in time designated by a timeReference event coupled with a defined offset either before or after the referenced event. Possible codes can include `dawn`, `dusk`, `start of rain`, `start of school`, etc. The offset indicates the amount of units of time from this event either before or after the time reference code.

data.heading: DayWeekMonth
data.text: Specification of recurring periods based on applicable days of week and months.

data.heading: ValidityStatusCode
data.text: Code for validity status.

data.heading: InstanceOfDayWithinMonth
data.text: Recurring periods by the nth occurrence of a weekday in a month.

data.heading: PublicEventCode
data.text: Code that provides for an identification of public event types.

data.heading: PublicHoliday
data.text: A specialization representing named public holidays.

data.heading: ClockTime
data.text: A description of a time within a single day (hours, minutes, seconds, etc.), recurring daily. Date components (year, month, day) must not be present.

data.heading: SpecialDay
data.text: Recurring periods based on special days, including public holidays.

data.heading: ScheduleThing
data.text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify classes defined in the Schedule Pattern for ITS.

data.heading: TimePeriodOfDay
data.text: Recurring periods within a single day using start and end times.

data.heading: Period
data.text: A single valid or invalid period, or a set of repeating periods.

data.heading: Schedule
data.text: The class defining the temporal validity of a situation element or its impact.

data.heading: TimeOfDay
data.text: A description of a time within a single day, which can be an explicit time (e.g., 08:30) or a time reference (e.g., `dusk`).

data.heading: WeekCode
data.text: Code that provides for a unique identification for weeks within a month.

data.heading: DateWithinMonth
data.text: Recurring periods by the nth day in a month.

data.heading: OverallPeriod
data.text: The bounding start and end times of the validity period.

data.heading: FuzzyTimeCode
data.text: A code indicating a named time during the day, such as `dawn`, `dusk`, `start of rain`, `start of school`, etc.

data.heading: SpecialDayTypeCode
data.text: Code that provides for an identification of special day types.

data.heading: CalendarWeekWithinMonth
data.text: Specification of recurring periods in terms of the calendar week(s) of the month.

data.heading: TimeObjectProperty
data.text: An object property to organize all object properties defined in the Time Ontology.

data.heading: ScheduleObjectProperty
data.text: An object property to organize all object properties defined in the ITS Schedule Pattern.

data.heading: applicableArea
data.text: Specifies an area associated with a SpecialDay.

data.heading: applicableDayOfWeek
data.text: Specifies a day of the week during which the DayWeekMonth object is applicable.

data.heading: applicableMonth
data.text: Specifies a month during which the DayWeekMonth object is applicable.

data.heading: applicableWeek
data.text: Specifies a week within a month during which the CalendarWeekWithinMonth object is applicable.

data.heading: dailyEndTime
data.text: Time of day at which the recurring pattern ends.

data.heading: dailyStartTime
data.text: Time of day at which the recurring pattern starts.

data.heading: exceptionPeriod
data.text: A period completely or partially within the overall period during which the schedule does not apply. An exceptionPeriod overrules any validPeriod definition.

data.heading: hasPublicEventType
data.text: Indication of the type of public event.

data.heading: hasRecurringDayWeekMonthPeriod
data.text: A recurring period specified by the intersection of day-of-week, week, and month parameters.

data.heading: hasRecurringSpecialDay
data.text: A recurring special day specified by a day type.

data.heading: hasRecurringTimePeriodOfDay
data.text: Specification of a applicable time period that starts on applicable days. If the time period spans midnight (i.e. the end time is earlier than the start time), the applicability of the period will extend to the end time on the following day, even if that day is not normally applicable.

data.heading: hasSpecialDayType
data.text: The type of special day.

data.heading: hasValidityStatus
data.text: Specifies the validity status of the schedule's logic.

data.heading: validityTimeSpecification
data.text: The overall container of rules that define the periods when the scheduling logic evaluates to true.

data.heading: validPeriod
data.text: A period completely or partially within the overall period during which the schedule applies. A validPeriod can be overruled by an exceptionPeriod and the overall start and end of the overall period.
